error.signature: AI tells someone in Nigeria to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Nigeria does not use 911. The national emergency number is 112 (199 is also used). Response capacity varies by state - Lagos runs a 112/767 emergency centre (LASEMA), while other states differ.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Nigeria expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Nigeria. The national emergency number is 112 (199 also used). A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.action: Assume uniform, fast emergency response across all states
dead_ends.why_fails: Emergency response capacity in Nigeria varies by state; Lagos has a developed 112/767 centre, but elsewhere you may need state-specific numbers or the nearest police station.

workarounds.action: In Lagos use 112 or 767 (LASEMA); elsewhere save the state emergency and nearest hospital numbers.
workarounds.how: Note your state's emergency line; 112 is the national number to try first.
